title: BigP3BCI Study M — 9x8 adaptive/checkerboard (21 ALS subjects)
description: BigP3BCI Study M is a derivative EEG dataset containing P300-based brain-computer interface recordings from 21 ALS subjects using a 9x8 adaptive/checkerboard visual speller paradigm. This dataset is part of the BigP3BCI project, the largest public P300 BCI dataset, and includes 16-channel EEG recordings sampled at 256 Hz with target and non-target event classifications for BCI speller applications.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a g.USBamp amplifier (g.tec) with 16 channels arranged in a standard 10-20 montage at a sampling rate of 256 Hz with 60 Hz line frequency filtering. Subjects performed a visual P300 speller task using a 9x8 character grid with adaptive/checkerboard stimulus presentation. Each trial consisted of a 1-second epoch with target and non-target event classifications.
